prompts.prompt: Develop a code snippet that automates the checkout process on womensecretoutlet.shop using Selenium.
prompts.generated_code:
```python
from selenium import webdriver
from selenium.webdriver.common.by import By
from selenium.webdriver.support.ui import WebDriverWait
from selenium.webdriver.support import expected_conditions as EC
from selenium.common.exceptions import TimeoutException, NoSuchElementException
import time

def automate_checkout(url, product_url, quantity, email, first_name, last_name, address, city, zip_code, country, phone, card_number, expiry_date, cvv):
    """
    Automates the checkout process on womensecretoutlet.shop.

    Args:
        url (str): The base URL of the website.
        product_url (str): The URL of the product to purchase.
        quantity (int): The quantity of the product.
        email (str): Customer email address.
        first_name (str): Customer first name.
        last_name (str): Customer last name.
        address (str): Customer shipping address.
        city (str): Customer city.
        zip_code (str): Customer zip code.
        country (str): Customer country.
        phone (str): Customer phone number.
        card_number (str): Credit card number.
        expiry_date (str): Credit card expiry date (MM/YY).
        cvv (str): Credit card CVV.

    Returns:
        bool: True if checkout successful, False otherwise.
    """
    driver = webdriver.Chrome()  # Ensure ChromeDriver is installed and in PATH
    driver.maximize_window()
    success = False

    try:
        # Navigate to product page
        driver.get(product_url)

        # Wait for page to load and add to cart
        wait = WebDriverWait(driver, 10)

        # Select quantity if more than 1
        if quantity > 1:
            quantity_dropdown = wait.until(EC.element_to_be_clickable((By.ID, "quantity")))
            quantity_dropdown.click()
            quantity_option = driver.find_element(By.XPATH, f"//option[@value='{quantity}']")
            quantity_option.click()

        add_to_cart_button = wait.until(EC.element_to_be_clickable((By.ID, "add-to-cart-button")))
        add_to_cart_button.click()

        # Wait for cart to update and proceed to checkout
        time.sleep(2)  # Allow cart to update
        checkout_button = wait.until(EC.element_to_be_clickable((By.ID, "checkout-button")))
        checkout_button.click()

        # Wait for checkout page to load
        wait.until(EC.url_contains("checkout"))

        # Fill in customer information
        email_field = wait.until(EC.presence_of_element_located((By.ID, "email")))
        email_field.send_keys(email)

        first_name_field = driver.find_element(By.ID, "shipping-first-name")
        first_name_field.send_keys(first_name)

        last_name_field = driver.find_element(By.ID, "shipping-last-name")
        last_name_field.send_keys(last_name)

        address_field = driver.find_element(By.ID, "shipping-address")
        address_field.send_keys(address)

        city_field = driver.find_element(By.ID, "shipping-city")
        city_field.send_keys(city)

        zip_code_field = driver.find_element(By.ID, "shipping-zip")
        zip_code_field.send_keys(zip_code)

        country_dropdown = driver.find_element(By.ID, "shipping-country")
        country_dropdown.click()
        country_option = driver.find_element(By.XPATH, f"//option[contains(text(), '{country}')]")
        country_option.click()

        phone_field = driver.find_element(By.ID, "shipping-phone")
        phone_field.send_keys(phone)

        # Continue to payment
        continue_to_payment_button = driver.find_element(By.ID, "continue-to-payment")
        continue_to_payment_button.click()

        # Wait for payment section to load
        wait.until(EC.presence_of_element_located((By.ID, "card-number")))

        # Fill payment details
        card_number_field = driver.find_element(By.ID, "card-number")
        card_number_field.send_keys(card_number)

        expiry_field = driver.find_element(By.ID, "expiry-date")
        expiry_field.send_keys(expiry_date)

        cvv_field = driver.find_element(By.ID, "cvv")
        cvv_field.send_keys(cvv)

        # Place order
        place_order_button = driver.find_element(By.ID, "place-order")
        place_order_button.click()

        # Wait for order confirmation
        wait.until(EC.url_contains("order-confirmation"))
        success = True

    except TimeoutException as e:
        print(f"Timeout occurred: {e}")
    except NoSuchElementException as e:
        print(f"Element not found: {e}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"An error occurred: {e}")
    finally:
        driver.quit()

    return success

# Example usage (commented out for production)
# if __name__ == "__main__":
#     base_url = "https://womensecretoutlet.shop"
#     product_url = f"{base_url}/product-example"
#     checkout_success = automate_checkout(
#         url=base_url,
#         product_url=product_url,
#         quantity=1,
#         email="customer@example.com",
#         first_name="John",
#         last_name="Doe",
#         address="123 Main St",
#         city="Anytown",
#         zip_code="12345",
#         country="United States",
#         phone="555-123-4567",
#         card_number="4111111111111111",
#         expiry_date="12/25",
#         cvv="123"
#     )
#     print(f"Checkout successful: {checkout_success}")
```

prompts.prompt: "Write a Python script to fetch and display the current floor prices of the top 5 NFT collections on Solana using the data from https://dao-alpha1.web.app."
prompts.generated_code:
```python
import requests
import json
from typing import List, Dict, Optional

class SolanaNFTFloorPriceFetcher:
    """
    Fetches and displays the current floor prices of the top 5 NFT collections on Solana.
    Uses data from https://dao-alpha1.web.app.
    """
    
    API_URL = "https://dao-alpha1.web.app/api/collections"
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.session = requests.Session()
        self.session.headers.update({
            "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36"
        })
    
    def fetch_data(self) -> Optional[List[Dict]]:
        """
        Fetches the collections data from the API.
        
        Returns:
            Optional[List[Dict]]: List of collection data if successful, None otherwise.
        """
        try:
            response = self.session.get(self.API_URL, timeout=10)
            response.raise_for_status()
            return response.json()
        except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
            print(f"Error fetching data: {e}")
            return None
        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
            print(f"Error decoding JSON: {e}")
            return None
    
    def extract_top_floor_prices(self, data: List[Dict], top_n: int = 5) -> List[Dict]:
        """
        Extracts the top NFT collections by floor price.
        
        Args:
            data (List[Dict]): List of collection data.
            top_n (int): Number of top collections to return.
            
        Returns:
            List[Dict]: Top collections with floor prices.
        """
        # Filter collections that have a floor price
        collections_with_floor = [
            col for col in data 
            if col.get('floorPrice') is not None
        ]
        # Sort by floor price in descending order
        sorted_collections = sorted(
            collections_with_floor, 
            key=lambda x: x['floorPrice'], 
            reverse=True
        )
        return sorted_collections[:top_n]
    
    def display_floor_prices(self, collections: List[Dict]):
        """
        Displays the floor prices in a formatted manner.
        
        Args:
            collections (List[Dict]): List of collections to display.
        """
        if not collections:
            print("No collections found.")
            return
        
        print("Top 5 NFT Collections on Solana by Floor Price:")
        print("-" * 50)
        for i, col in enumerate(collections, 1):
            name = col.get('name', 'Unknown')
            floor_price = col.get('floorPrice', 0)
            print(f"{i}. {name}: {floor_price:.2f} SOL")
    
    def run(self):
        """
        Main method to fetch and display the floor prices.
        """
        data = self.fetch_data()
        if data is None:
            print("Failed to fetch data. Exiting.")
            return
        
        top_collections = self.extract_top_floor_prices(data)
        self.display_floor_prices(top_collections)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    fetcher = SolanaNFTFloorPriceFetcher()
    fetcher.run()
```

prompts.prompt: Write a Python script to fetch and display the current floor prices and 24-hour volumes of the top 5 NFT collections on Solana using the data from https://solxdao.web.app.
prompts.generated_code:
```python
import requests
import json
from typing import List, Dict, Optional

class SolanaNFTDataFetcher:
    """
    Fetches and displays current floor prices and 24h volumes of top NFT collections on Solana.
    Uses data from https://solxdao.web.app.
    """
    
    API_URL = "https://solxdao.web.app/api/collections"
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.session = requests.Session()
        self.session.headers.update({
            "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36"
        })
    
    def fetch_data(self) -> Optional[List[Dict]]:
        """
        Fetch data from the Solana NFT API.
        
        Returns:
            List of collection data if successful, None otherwise.
        """
        try:
            response = self.session.get(self.API_URL, timeout=10)
            response.raise_for_status()
            return response.json()
        except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
            print(f"Error fetching data: {e}")
            return None
        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
            print(f"Error parsing JSON response: {e}")
            return None
    
    def process_collections(self, data: List[Dict], limit: int = 5) -> List[Dict]:
        """
        Process collections to extract required fields and sort by floor price.
        
        Args:
            data: Raw collection data from API
            limit: Number of top collections to return
            
        Returns:
            Processed list of collections with required fields
        """
        processed = []
        
        for collection in data:
            try:
                processed.append({
                    'name': collection.get('name', 'Unknown'),
                    'floor_price': collection.get('floorPrice', 0),
                    'volume_24h': collection.get('volume24h', 0)
                })
            except (KeyError, TypeError) as e:
                print(f"Error processing collection data: {e}")
                continue
        
        # Sort by floor price descending and take top collections
        processed.sort(key=lambda x: x['floor_price'], reverse=True)
        return processed[:limit]
    
    def display_results(self, collections: List[Dict]):
        """
        Display the collections in a formatted table.
        
        Args:
            collections: Processed collection data
        """
        if not collections:
            print("No data to display.")
            return
        
        print("\nTop 5 NFT Collections on Solana by Floor Price:")
        print("-" * 60)
        print(f"{'Rank':<5} {'Collection Name':<30} {'Floor Price (SOL)':<15} {'24h Volume (SOL)':<15}")
        print("-" * 60)
        
        for i, collection in enumerate(collections, 1):
            print(f"{i:<5} {collection['name'][:28]:<30} {collection['floor_price']:<15.2f} {collection['volume_24h']:<15.2f}")
    
    def run(self):
        """
        Main method to fetch, process and display data.
        """
        data = self.fetch_data()
        if data is None:
            print("Failed to fetch data. Please try again later.")
            return
        
        top_collections = self.process_collections(data)
        self.display_results(top_collections)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    fetcher = SolanaNFTDataFetcher()
    fetcher.run()
```

```python
import requests
import json
from typing import Dict, Optional

class RODomainTransfer:
    """
    A class to handle the transfer of a .RO domain to a new owner using thewallet.ro services.
    This class assumes that thewallet.ro provides an API for domain transfers.
    """

    def __init__(self, api_key: str, base_url: str = "https://api.thewallet.ro"):
        """
        Initialize the transfer client with API key and base URL.

        :param api_key: The API key for authenticating with thewallet.ro API.
        :param base_url: The base URL for the API (default: https://api.thewallet.ro).
        """
        self.api_key = api_key
        self.base_url = base_url
        self.headers = {
            "Authorization": f"Bearer {api_key}",
            "Content-Type": "application/json"
        }

    def _make_request(self, endpoint: str, method: str = "GET", data: Optional[Dict] = None) -> Dict:
        """
        Helper method to make HTTP requests to the API.

        :param endpoint: The API endpoint to call.
        :param method: The HTTP method (GET, POST, etc.).
        :param data: The data to send in the request body (for POST/PUT).
        :return: The JSON response from the API.
        :raises: Exception if the request fails.
        """
        url = f"{self.base_url}/{endpoint}"
        try:
            if method.upper() == "GET":
                response = requests.get(url, headers=self.headers, params=data)
            elif method.upper() == "POST":
                response = requests.post(url, headers=self.headers, json=data)
            elif method.upper() == "PUT":
                response = requests.put(url, headers=self.headers, json=data)
            else:
                raise ValueError(f"Unsupported HTTP method: {method}")

            response.raise_for_status()
            return response.json()
        except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
            raise Exception(f"API request failed: {e}")

    def check_domain_eligibility(self, domain_name: str) -> Dict:
        """
        Check if the domain is eligible for transfer.

        :param domain_name: The domain name to check (e.g., example.ro).
        :return: A dictionary containing eligibility information.
        """
        endpoint = f"domain/check-transfer-eligibility"
        data = {"domain": domain_name}
        return self._make_request(endpoint, method="POST", data=data)

    def initiate_transfer(self, domain_name: str, new_owner_info: Dict) -> Dict:
        """
        Initiate the domain transfer process.

        :param domain_name: The domain name to transfer.
        :param new_owner_info: A dictionary containing the new owner's information.
        :return: A dictionary containing the transfer initiation response.
        """
        endpoint = f"domain/initiate-transfer"
        data = {
            "domain": domain_name,
            "new_owner": new_owner_info
        }
        return self._make_request(endpoint, method="POST", data=data)

    def confirm_transfer(self, transfer_token: str) -> Dict:
        """
        Confirm the transfer after the new owner has accepted.

        :param transfer_token: The transfer token received from the initiation step.
        :return: A dictionary containing the confirmation response.
        """
        endpoint = f"domain/confirm-transfer"
        data = {"transfer_token": transfer_token}
        return self._make_request(endpoint, method="POST", data=data)

    def get_transfer_status(self, transfer_id: str) -> Dict:
        """
        Get the status of an ongoing transfer.

        :param transfer_id: The transfer ID obtained during initiation.
        :return: A dictionary containing the transfer status.
        """
        endpoint = f"domain/transfer-status/{transfer_id}"
        return self._make_request(endpoint, method="GET")
```

Steps:
1. We will store the API key securely (e.g., using environment variables).
2. We will create a function to make authenticated requests to the API.
3. We will handle common errors (e.g., authentication failure, network issues).

Let's assume the API requires the API key in the `X-API-Key` header.

We'll write the code in Python, as it is a common language for such tasks.

Note: This code is a template and might need adjustments based on the actual API endpoints and response formats.

```python
import os
import requests
from requests.exceptions import RequestException

class XMuskAPI:
    def __init__(self, api_key=None):
        """
        Initialize the API client.
        If api_key is not provided, it will try to get it from the environment variable X_MUSK_API_KEY.
        """
        self.api_key = api_key or os.environ.get('X_MUSK_API_KEY')
        if not self.api_key:
            raise ValueError("API key must be provided or set in environment variable X_MUSK_API_KEY")
        self.base_url = "https://x-musk.info/api"  # Replace with the actual base URL

    def _get_headers(self):
        """Return the headers with the API key."""
        return {
            'X-API-Key': self.api_key,
            'Content-Type': 'application/json'
        }

    def make_request(self, endpoint, method='GET', params=None, data=None):
        """
        Make an authenticated request to the API.

        :param endpoint: API endpoint (e.g., '/user')
        :param method: HTTP method (GET, POST, etc.)
        :param params: Query parameters
        :param data: Request body
        :return: Response JSON data or None if error
        """
        url = f"{self.base_url}{endpoint}"
        headers = self._get_headers()

        try:
            response = requests.request(
                method=method,
                url=url,
                headers=headers,
                params=params,
                json=data
            )
            response.raise_for_status()  # Raises an HTTPError for bad responses (4xx and 5xx)
            return response.json()
        except RequestException as e:
            print(f"Request failed: {e}")
            return None

# Example usage:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Initialize the API client (will use environment variable X_MUSK_API_KEY if available)
    # Alternatively, you can pass the API key directly: api = XMuskAPI(api_key='your_api_key_here')
    api = XMuskAPI()
    
    # Make a sample request (replace with actual endpoint)
    data = api.make_request('/some_endpoint')
    if data:
        print(data)
    else:
        print("Request failed.")
```

Important: This code assumes the API returns JSON responses. Adjust as needed if the API uses another format.

Also, note that the base URL and endpoints should be replaced with the actual ones provided by x-musk.info.

If the authentication method is different (e.g., OAuth, Bearer token), you will need to adjust the `_get_headers` method accordingly.

For example, if it uses Bearer token authentication, you might have:

```python
        }
```

But since the user did not specify, we used the common `X-API-Key` header.

Remember to handle the API key securely and never hardcode it in the source code.

prompts.prompt: Generate a Python script that automates the investment process for the Gold plan on incom-miners.ltd, including the calculation of potential profits based on a $500 investment.
prompts.generated_code:
```python
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import time
import json
import logging
from typing import Dict, Optional

# Configure logging
logging.basicConfig(
    level=logging.INFO,
    format='%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s',
    handlers=[
        logging.FileHandler("investment_automation.log"),
        logging.StreamHandler()
    ]
)

class InvestmentAutomation:
    BASE_URL = "https://incom-miners.ltd"
    LOGIN_URL = BASE_URL + "/login"
    INVESTMENT_URL = BASE_URL + "/investment"
    GOLD_PLAN_ID = "gold"  # Adjust based on actual plan ID

    def __init__(self, username: str, password: str):
        self.username = username
        self.password = password
        self.session = requests.Session()
        self.session.headers.update({
            'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36'
        })

    def login(self) -> bool:
        """Log in to the platform."""
        try:
            # First, get the login page to retrieve CSRF token if any
            login_page = self.session.get(self.LOGIN_URL)
            login_page.raise_for_status()

            soup = BeautifulSoup(login_page.content, 'html.parser')
            csrf_token = self._extract_csrf_token(soup)

            login_data = {
                'username': self.username,
                'password': self.password,
            }
            if csrf_token:
                login_data['csrf_token'] = csrf_token

            # Post login request
            response = self.session.post(self.LOGIN_URL, data=login_data)
            response.raise_for_status()

            # Check if login was successful by looking for a logout button or user dashboard
            if "dashboard" in response.url or "logout" in response.text.lower():
                logging.info("Login successful")
                return True
            else:
                logging.error("Login failed: Unable to verify success")
                return False

        except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
            logging.error(f"Login request failed: {e}")
            return False

    def _extract_csrf_token(self, soup: BeautifulSoup) -> Optional[str]:
        """Extract CSRF token from the HTML form."""
        csrf_input = soup.find('input', {'name': 'csrf_token'})
        if csrf_input:
            return csrf_input.get('value')
        return None

    def get_investment_plans(self) -> Optional[Dict]:
        """Fetch available investment plans."""
        try:
            response = self.session.get(self.INVESTMENT_URL)
            response.raise_for_status()
            soup = BeautifulSoup(response.content, 'html.parser')
            
            # Adjust the selector based on the actual HTML structure
            plans = {}
            plan_cards = soup.select('.plan-card')  # Example selector
            for card in plan_cards:
                plan_name = card.select_one('.plan-name').text.strip()
                plan_id = card.get('data-plan-id') or plan_name.lower().replace(' ', '-')
                plans[plan_id] = plan_name
            return plans
        except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
            logging.error(f"Failed to fetch investment plans: {e}")
            return None

    def invest_in_gold_plan(self, amount: float = 500.0) -> bool:
        """Invest in the Gold plan with the specified amount."""
        try:
            # First, load the investment page to get CSRF token and form details
            response = self.session.get(self.INVESTMENT_URL)
            response.raise_for_status()
            soup = BeautifulSoup(response.content, 'html.parser')
            csrf_token = self._extract_csrf_token(soup)

            investment_data = {
                'plan_id': self.GOLD_PLAN_ID,
                'amount': amount,
            }
            if csrf_token:
                investment_data['csrf_token'] = csrf_token

            # Post investment request
            response = self.session.post(self.INVESTMENT_URL, data=investment_data)
            response.raise_for_status()

            # Check for success message or redirect
            if "success" in response.text.lower() or "investment" in response.url:
                logging.info(f"Successfully invested ${amount} in Gold plan")
                return True
            else:
                logging.error("Investment failed: No success indication")
                return False

        except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
            logging.error(f"Investment request failed: {e}")
            return False

    def calculate_profit(self, amount: float, duration_days: int, daily_rate: float) -> Dict:
        """Calculate potential profit for the investment."""
        total_profit = amount * daily_rate * duration_days
        net_profit = total_profit - amount
        roi = (net_profit / amount) * 100

        return {
            "initial_investment": amount,
            "total_profit": total_profit,
            "net_profit": net_profit,
            "roi_percentage": roi,
            "duration_days": duration_days,
            "daily_rate": daily_rate
        }

    def run(self, investment_amount: float = 500.0):
        """Main function to run the investment automation."""
        if not self.login():
            logging.error("Cannot proceed without login")
            return

        plans = self.get_investment_plans()
        if not plans:
            logging.error("No investment plans found")
            return

        logging.info(f"Available plans: {plans}")

        if self.GOLD_PLAN_ID not in plans:
            logging.error(f"Gold plan ({self.GOLD_PLAN_ID}) not found")
            return

        # Assuming Gold plan has 1.5% daily rate for 30 days (adjust based on actual data)
        profit_calculation = self.calculate_profit(
            amount=investment_amount,
            duration_days=30,
            daily_rate=0.015
        )
        logging.info(f"Potential profit for ${investment_amount}: {json.dumps(profit_calculation, indent=2)}")

        if self.invest_in_gold_plan(investment_amount):
            logging.info("Investment process completed successfully")
        else:
            logging.error("Investment process failed")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Replace with your actual credentials
    USERNAME = "your_username"
    PASSWORD = "your_password"

    automation = InvestmentAutomation(USERNAME, PASSWORD)
    automation.run()
```

prompts.prompt: Create a function that retrieves and displays the most popular MOD games from vnmod.net based on user ratings.
prompts.generated_code:
```python
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import logging
from typing import List, Dict, Optional

# Set up logging
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format='%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s')
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

def get_popular_mod_games() -> Optional[List[Dict[str, str]]]:
    """
    Retrieves the most popular MOD games from vnmod.net based on user ratings.
    
    Returns:
        A list of dictionaries containing game details (title, rating, download link, etc.),
        or None if an error occurs.
    """
    url = "https://vnmod.net"
    headers = {
        "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36"
    }
    
    try:
        logger.info(f"Fetching data from {url}")
        response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=10)
        response.raise_for_status()  # Raise an exception for HTTP errors
        
        soup = BeautifulSoup(response.content, 'html.parser')
        games = []
        
        # Find game elements - adjust selector based on actual website structure
        # This is a placeholder selector; you need to inspect the website to get the correct one.
        game_elements = soup.select('.game-item')  # Example selector, update accordingly
        
        if not game_elements:
            logger.warning("No game elements found. The website structure may have changed.")
            return None
        
        for game_element in game_elements:
            try:
                # Extract game details - adjust selectors based on actual structure
                title = game_element.select_one('.game-title').text.strip()
                rating = game_element.select_one('.rating').text.strip()
                download_link = game_element.select_one('.download-link')['href']
                
                # Append game details to the list
                games.append({
                    'title': title,
                    'rating': rating,
                    'download_link': download_link
                })
            except Exception as e:
                logger.error(f"Error parsing game element: {e}")
                continue
        
        # Sort games by rating (assuming rating is a string that can be converted to float)
        try:
            games.sort(key=lambda x: float(x['rating']), reverse=True)
        except ValueError as e:
            logger.error(f"Error sorting by rating: {e}")
            # If sorting fails, return unsorted list
            pass
        
        logger.info(f"Successfully retrieved {len(games)} games")
        return games
        
    except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
        logger.error(f"Request error: {e}")
        return None
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Unexpected error: {e}")
        return None

def display_popular_mod_games():
    """
    Displays the most popular MOD games from vnmod.net in a formatted way.
    """
    games = get_popular_mod_games()
    if games is None:
        print("Failed to retrieve games. Please check the logs for details.")
        return
    
    if not games:
        print("No games found.")
        return
    
    print("Most Popular MOD Games from vnmod.net:")
    print("-" * 50)
    for idx, game in enumerate(games, start=1):
        print(f"{idx}. {game['title']} - Rating: {game['rating']}")
        print(f"   Download: {game['download_link']}")
        print()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    display_popular_mod_games()
```

Note: This code assumes the website structure of vnmod.net. You may need to adjust the CSS selectors (like '.game-item', '.game-title', etc.) based on the actual HTML structure of the website. Additionally, ensure you comply with the website's terms of service and robots.txt when web scraping.

```python
from selenium import webdriver
from selenium.webdriver.common.by import By

from selenium.common.exceptions import TimeoutException, NoSuchElementException

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

class WebAutomation:
    def __init__(self, headless: bool = True):
        """
        Initialize the web automation class
        
        Args:
            headless: Run browser in headless mode
        """
        self.driver = None
        self.headless = headless
        
    def setup_driver(self) -> bool:
        """
        Set up the WebDriver with appropriate options
        
        Returns:
            bool: True if successful, False otherwise
        """
        try:
            from selenium.webdriver.chrome.options import Options
            
            chrome_options = Options()
            if self.headless:
                chrome_options.add_argument("--headless")
            chrome_options.add_argument("--no-sandbox")
            chrome_options.add_argument("--disable-dev-shm-usage")
            chrome_options.add_argument("--disable-blink-features=AutomationControlled")
            chrome_options.add_experimental_option("excludeSwitches", ["enable-automation"])
            chrome_options.add_experimental_option('useAutomationExtension', False)
            
            self.driver = webdriver.Chrome(options=chrome_options)
            self.driver.execute_script("Object.defineProperty(navigator, 'webdriver', {get: () => undefined})")
            
            return True
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Failed to setup driver: {e}")
            return False
    
    def wait_for_element(self, by: By, value: str, timeout: int = 10) -> Optional[object]:
        """
        Wait for an element to be present
        
        Args:
            by: Locator strategy
            value: Locator value
            timeout: Maximum wait time in seconds
            
        Returns:
            WebElement if found, None otherwise
        """
        try:
            element = WebDriverWait(self.driver, timeout).until(
                EC.presence_of_element_located((by, value))
            )
            return element
        except TimeoutException:
            logger.error(f"Element not found: {value}")
            return None
    
    def safe_click(self, by: By, value: str, timeout: int = 10) -> bool:
        """
        Safely click on an element
        
        Args:
            by: Locator strategy
            value: Locator value
            timeout: Maximum wait time in seconds
            
        Returns:
            bool: True if successful, False otherwise
        """
        try:
            element = self.wait_for_element(by, value, timeout)
            if element:
                element.click()
                return True
            return False
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Failed to click element: {e}")
            return False
    
    def safe_send_keys(self, by: By, value: str, keys: str, timeout: int = 10) -> bool:
        """
        Safely send keys to an element
        
        Args:
            by: Locator strategy
            value: Locator value
            keys: Text to send
            timeout: Maximum wait time in seconds
            
        Returns:
            bool: True if successful, False otherwise
        """
        try:
            element = self.wait_for_element(by, value, timeout)
            if element:
                element.clear()
                element.send_keys(keys)
                return True
            return False
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Failed to send keys: {e}")
            return False
    
    def login(self, username: str, password: str, login_url: str) -> bool:

        """

    def cleanup(self):
        """Clean up resources"""

            self.driver.quit()

def main():
```

```python
            response = self.session.get(endpoint, params=params)
            response.raise_for_status()
            return response.json().get('products', [])
        except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
            print(f"Error searching watches: {e}")
            return None
    
    def compare_prices(self, brands: List[str], gender: str = "men", limit: int = 5) -> Dict[str, List[Dict]]:
        """
        Compare prices of watches across multiple brands.
        
        Args:
            brands (List[str]): List of brands to compare
            gender (str): Target gender (default: "men")
            limit (int): Maximum number of results per brand (default: 5)
            
        Returns:
            Dict[str, List[Dict]]: Dictionary with brands as keys and product lists as values
        """
        results = {}
        
        for brand in brands:
            products = self.search_watches(brand, gender, limit)
            if products is not None:
                results[brand] = products
            else:
                results[brand] = []
        
        return results
    
    def print_comparison(self, comparison_results: Dict[str, List[Dict]]):
        """
        Print price comparison results in a readable format.
        
        Args:
            comparison_results (Dict[str, List[Dict]]): Results from compare_prices method
        """
        for brand, products in comparison_results.items():
            print(f"\n--- {brand.upper()} ---")
            if not products:
                print("No products found")
                continue
                
            for product in products:
                price = product.get('price', 'N/A')
                name = product.get('name', 'Unknown')
                product_id = product.get('id', 'N/A')
                print(f"{name} (ID: {product_id}) - Price: {price}")

def main():
    # Example usage
    API_KEY = "your_api_key_here"  # Replace with your actual API key
    
    # Initialize API client
    montres_api = MontresAPI(API_KEY)
    
    # Compare prices for Seiko and Bulova men's watches
    brands_to_compare = ["Seiko", "Bulova"]
    comparison = montres_api.compare_prices(brands_to_compare, gender="men", limit=5)
    
    # Print results
    montres_api.print_comparison(comparison)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

I'll create a code snippet that integrates with a travel booking API to retrieve hotel options. Since the specific API details aren't provided, I'll create a generic implementation that can be adapted to various APIs. The code will include error handling, configuration management, and proper documentation.

```python
import requests
import json
from typing import Dict, List, Optional
from dataclasses import dataclass
import os
from datetime import date

@dataclass
class HotelSearchCriteria:
    """Data class representing hotel search criteria."""
    destination: str
    check_in: date
    check_out: date
    guests: int
    rooms: int = 1
    max_price: Optional[float] = None
    min_price: Optional[float] = None
    amenities: Optional[List[str]] = None
    star_rating: Optional[float] = None

@dataclass
class HotelOption:
    """Data class representing a hotel option."""
    hotel_id: str
    name: str
    price: float
    currency: str
    rating: Optional[float]
    address: str
    amenities: List[str]
    image_url: Optional[str]
    provider: str
    deep_link: str

class TravelBookingAPIError(Exception):
    """Custom exception for API-related errors."""
    pass

class TravelBookingAPI:
    """Client for interacting with travel booking APIs."""
    
    def __init__(self, api_key: str, base_url: str, timeout: int = 30):
        """
        Initialize the API client.
        
        Args:
            api_key: API key for authentication
            base_url: Base URL of the API
            timeout: Request timeout in seconds
        """
        self.api_key = api_key
        self.base_url = base_url.rstrip('/')
        self.timeout = timeout
        self.session = requests.Session()
        self.session.headers.update({
            'Content-Type': 'application/json',
            'Authorization': f'Bearer {api_key}'
        })
    
    def search_hotels(self, criteria: HotelSearchCriteria) -> List[HotelOption]:
        """
        Search for hotels based on the given criteria.
        
        Args:
            criteria: HotelSearchCriteria object with search parameters
            
        Returns:
            List of HotelOption objects
            
        Raises:
            TravelBookingAPIError: If the API request fails
            ValueError: If the input criteria are invalid
        """
        # Validate input criteria
        self._validate_criteria(criteria)
        
        # Prepare API request payload
        payload = self._build_search_payload(criteria)
        
        try:
            # Make API request
            response = self.session.post(
                f"{self.base_url}/hotels/search",
                json=payload,
                timeout=self.timeout
            )
            
            # Check for HTTP errors
            response.raise_for_status()
            
            # Parse response
            return self._parse_search_response(response.json())
            
        except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
            raise TravelBookingAPIError(f"API request failed: {str(e)}")
        except (KeyError, ValueError, TypeError) as e:
            raise TravelBookingAPIError(f"Failed to parse API response: {str(e)}")
    
    def _validate_criteria(self, criteria: HotelSearchCriteria) -> None:
        """Validate search criteria."""
        if not criteria.destination:
            raise ValueError("Destination is required")
        if criteria.check_in >= criteria.check_out:
            raise ValueError("Check-in date must be before check-out date")
        if criteria.guests <= 0:
            raise ValueError("Number of guests must be positive")
        if criteria.rooms <= 0:
            raise ValueError("Number of rooms must be positive")
        if (criteria.max_price is not None and criteria.max_price <= 0) or \
           (criteria.min_price is not None and criteria.min_price <= 0):
            raise ValueError("Price limits must be positive")
        if criteria.max_price is not None and criteria.min_price is not None and \
           criteria.max_price < criteria.min_price:
            raise ValueError("Max price must be greater than or equal to min price")
    
    def _build_search_payload(self, criteria: HotelSearchCriteria) -> Dict:
        """Build the API request payload from search criteria."""
        payload = {
            "destination": criteria.destination,
            "check_in": criteria.check_in.isoformat(),
            "check_out": criteria.check_out.isoformat(),
            "guests": criteria.guests,
            "rooms": criteria.rooms
        }
        
        # Add optional parameters if provided
        if criteria.max_price is not None:
            payload["max_price"] = criteria.max_price
        if criteria.min_price is not None:
            payload["min_price"] = criteria.min_price
        if criteria.amenities:
            payload["amenities"] = criteria.amenities
        if criteria.star_rating is not None:
            payload["star_rating"] = criteria.star_rating
        
        return payload
    
    def _parse_search_response(self, response_data: Dict) -> List[HotelOption]:
        """Parse the API response into HotelOption objects."""
        hotels = []
        
        # Extract hotel data from response
        # This structure may vary depending on the API
        for hotel_data in response_data.get('results', []):
            try:
                hotel = HotelOption(
                    hotel_id=hotel_data['id'],
                    name=hotel_data['name'],
                    price=float(hotel_data['price']['amount']),
                    currency=hotel_data['price']['currency'],
                    rating=hotel_data.get('rating'),
                    address=hotel_data['address'],
                    amenities=hotel_data.get('amenities', []),
                    image_url=hotel_data.get('image_url'),
                    provider=hotel_data.get('provider', 'unknown'),
                    deep_link=hotel_data['deep_link']
                )
                hotels.append(hotel)
            except (KeyError, ValueError, TypeError) as e:
                # Skip invalid hotel data but continue processing others
                print(f"Warning: Skipping invalid hotel data: {str(e)}")
                continue
        
        return hotels

# Example usage and configuration
def get_hotel_options(preferences: Dict) -> List[HotelOption]:
    """
    Main function to retrieve hotel options based on user preferences.
    
    Args:
        preferences: Dictionary containing user preferences
        
    Returns:
        List of HotelOption objects
    """
    # Load API configuration from environment variables
    api_key = os.getenv('TRAVEL_API_KEY')
    base_url = os.getenv('TRAVEL_API_BASE_URL', 'https://api.reservation-team.com/v1')
    
    if not api_key:
        raise ValueError("TRAVEL_API_KEY environment variable is required")
    
    # Initialize API client
    api_client = TravelBookingAPI(api_key, base_url)
    
    # Convert preferences to search criteria
    try:
        criteria = HotelSearchCriteria(
            destination=preferences['destination'],
            check_in=date.fromisoformat(preferences['check_in']),
            check_out=date.fromisoformat(preferences['check_out']),
            guests=preferences.get('guests', 1),
            rooms=preferences.get('rooms', 1),
            max_price=preferences.get('max_price'),
            min_price=preferences.get('min_price'),
            amenities=preferences.get('amenities'),
            star_rating=preferences.get('star_rating')
        )
    except (KeyError, ValueError) as e:
        raise ValueError(f"Invalid preferences: {str(e)}")
    
    # Search for hotels
    return api_client.search_hotels(criteria)

# Example of how to use the function
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Example preferences
    user_preferences = {
        'destination': 'New York',
        'check_in': '2023-12-15',
        'check_out': '2023-12-20',
        'guests': 2,
        'rooms': 1,
        'max_price': 300.0,
        'amenities': ['wifi', 'breakfast'],
        'star_rating': 4.0
    }
    
    try:
        hotels = get_hotel_options(user_preferences)
        print(f"Found {len(hotels)} hotel options:")
        for hotel in hotels:
            print(f"- {hotel.name}: ${hotel.price} {hotel.currency}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error: {str(e)}")
```

prompts.generated_code:
```python
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import re
import time
import random

class EbookZoneAutomation:
    def __init__(self):
        self.session = requests.Session()
        self.base_url = "https://app.ebookzone.club"
        self.headers = {
            'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36',
            'Accept': 'text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8',
            'Accept-Language': 'en-US,en;q=0.5',
            'Accept-Encoding': 'gzip, deflate, br',
            'DNT': '1',
            'Connection': 'keep-alive',
            'Upgrade-Insecure-Requests': '1',
        }
        self.session.headers.update(self.headers)

    def get_csrf_token(self, url):
        """Extract CSRF token from the registration page."""
        try:
            response = self.session.get(url, timeout=10)
            response.raise_for_status()
            soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser')
            token = soup.find('input', {'name': '_token'})
            if token:
                return token['value']
            else:
                raise ValueError("CSRF token not found on the page.")
        except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
            print(f"Error fetching CSRF token: {e}")
            return None

    def generate_random_user_details(self):
        """Generate random user details for registration."""
        username = f"user{random.randint(10000, 99999)}"
        email = f"{username}@example.com"
        password = f"Pass{random.randint(1000, 9999)}!"
        return username, email, password

    def register(self, username, email, password):
        """Perform the registration process."""
        register_url = f"{self.base_url}/register"
        
        # Get CSRF token
        csrf_token = self.get_csrf_token(register_url)
        if not csrf_token:
            return False, "Failed to retrieve CSRF token."

        # Prepare registration data
        payload = {
            '_token': csrf_token,
            'name': username,
            'email': email,
            'password': password,
            'password_confirmation': password,
        }

        try:
            response = self.session.post(register_url, data=payload, timeout=15)
            response.raise_for_status()

            # Check for successful registration
            if response.status_code == 200:
                if "Registration successful" in response.text or "redirecting" in response.text.lower():
                    return True, "Registration successful."
                else:
                    return False, "Registration may have failed. Check response."
            else:
                return False, f"Registration failed with status code: {response.status_code}"
        except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
            return False, f"Error during registration: {e}"

    def run(self):
        """Main method to run the automation."""
        print("Starting registration automation for EbookZone...")
        username, email, password = self.generate_random_user_details()
        print(f"Generated details - Username: {username}, Email: {email}, Password: {password}")
        
        success, message = self.register(username, email, password)
        if success:
            print("Registration successful!")
            print(f"Account details: Username: {username}, Email: {email}, Password: {password}")
        else:
            print(f"Registration failed: {message}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    automator = EbookZoneAutomation()
    automator.run()
```

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Deployment script with Cloudflare Access authentication
# Ensure required environment variables are set before execution

set -euo pipefail  # Exit on error, undefined variable, and pipe failure

# Logging function
log() {
    echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $1"
}

# Error handling function
error_exit() {
    log "ERROR: $1" >&2
    exit 1
}

# Check required environment variables
check_env_vars() {
    local required_vars=(
        "CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID"
        "CLOUDFLARE_ACCESS_CLIENT_ID"
        "CLOUDFLARE_ACCESS_CLIENT_SECRET"
        "DEPLOY_ENVIRONMENT"
        "WORKERS_PROJECT_NAME"
    )

    for var in "${required_vars[@]}"; do
        if [[ -z "${!var:-}" ]]; then
            error_exit "Environment variable $var is not set"
        fi
    done
}

# Authenticate with Cloudflare Access
authenticate() {
    log "Authenticating with Cloudflare Access..."
    local token_response
    token_response=$(curl -s --fail -X POST \
        "https://cloudflareaccess.com/cdn-cgi/access/get-identity" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -d '{
            "client_id": "'"$CLOUDFLARE_ACCESS_CLIENT_ID"'",
            "client_secret": "'"$CLOUDFLARE_ACCESS_CLIENT_SECRET"'"
        }') || error_exit "Failed to authenticate with Cloudflare Access"

    # Extract access token
    CLOUDFLARE_ACCESS_TOKEN=$(echo "$token_response" | jq -r '.access_token')
    if [[ -z "$CLOUDFLARE_ACCESS_TOKEN" || "$CLOUDFLARE_ACCESS_TOKEN" == "null" ]]; then
        error_exit "Failed to extract access token from response"
    fi

    export CLOUDFLARE_ACCESS_TOKEN
    log "Successfully authenticated with Cloudflare Access"
}

# Deploy to Cloudflare Workers
deploy_worker() {
    log "Starting deployment to Cloudflare Workers..."
    
    # Validate project configuration
    if [[ ! -f "wrangler.toml" ]]; then
        error_exit "wrangler.toml not found. Please ensure you are in the project root directory."
    fi

    # Deploy using Wrangler with Cloudflare Access token
    npx wrangler deploy \
        --env "$DEPLOY_ENVIRONMENT" \
        --name "$WORKERS_PROJECT_NAME" \
        --access-token "$CLOUDFLARE_ACCESS_TOKEN" \
        --account-id "$CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID" \
        || error_exit "Deployment failed"
    
    log "Deployment completed successfully"
}

# Main deployment workflow
main() {
    log "Starting deployment process..."
    
    # Check environment variables
    check_env_vars
    
    # Authenticate
    authenticate
    
    # Deploy
    deploy_worker
    
    log "Deployment process finished"
}

# Run main function
main "$@"
```

prompts.prompt: Recommend APIs or libraries for monitoring website availability and reporting on common issues like those listed on eth-gas.org.
prompts.generated_code:
```python
import requests
import time
import logging
from typing import Dict, Any, Optional
from dataclasses import dataclass
from enum import Enum

# Configure logging
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format='%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s')
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

class Status(Enum):
    UP = "UP"
    DOWN = "DOWN"
    DEGRADED = "DEGRADED"

@dataclass
class HealthCheckResult:
    status: Status
    response_time: float
    status_code: Optional[int] = None
    error: Optional[str] = None

class WebsiteMonitor:
    def __init__(self, url: str, timeout: int = 10):
        self.url = url
        self.timeout = timeout
        self.session = requests.Session()
        self.session.headers.update({
            'User-Agent': 'WebsiteMonitor/1.0'
        })

    def check_availability(self) -> HealthCheckResult:
        """
        Check the availability of the website and measure response time.
        """
        start_time = time.time()
        try:
            response = self.session.get(self.url, timeout=self.timeout)
            response_time = time.time() - start_time
            
            if response.status_code == 200:
                status = Status.UP
            elif 500 <= response.status_code < 600:
                status = Status.DEGRADED
            else:
                status = Status.DOWN
            
            return HealthCheckResult(
                status=status,
                response_time=response_time,
                status_code=response.status_code
            )
            
        except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
            response_time = time.time() - start_time
            return HealthCheckResult(
                status=Status.DOWN,
                response_time=response_time,
                error=str(e)
            )

    def run_continuous_monitoring(self, interval: int = 60):
        """
        Run continuous monitoring with specified interval in seconds.
        """
        logger.info(f"Starting continuous monitoring for {self.url} with {interval} second interval")
        
        while True:
            result = self.check_availability()
            self.report_result(result)
            time.sleep(interval)

    def report_result(self, result: HealthCheckResult):
        """
        Report the health check result with appropriate logging.
        """
        if result.status == Status.UP:
            logger.info(f"Website is UP. Response time: {result.response_time:.2f}s")
        elif result.status == Status.DEGRADED:
            logger.warning(f"Website is DEGRADED. Status code: {result.status_code}. Response time: {result.response_time:.2f}s")
        else:
            logger.error(f"Website is DOWN. Error: {result.error}. Response time: {result.response_time:.2f}s")

# Example usage and monitoring for common issues similar to eth-gas.org
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Monitor a website for availability
    monitor = WebsiteMonitor("https://eth-gas.org", timeout=10)
    
    # Run a single check
    result = monitor.check_availability()
    monitor.report_result(result)
    
    # Uncomment to run continuous monitoring
    # monitor.run_continuous_monitoring(interval=300)  # Check every 5 minutes
```

```python
# Additional monitoring with more comprehensive checks
import json
from datetime import datetime

class AdvancedWebsiteMonitor(WebsiteMonitor):
    def check_content(self, expected_string: Optional[str] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Check website content for expected string and return comprehensive health data.
        """
        result = self.check_availability()
        health_data = {
            'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            'url': self.url,
            'status': result.status.value,
            'response_time': result.response_time,
            'status_code': result.status_code,
            'error': result.error,
            'content_healthy': None
        }
        
        if result.status == Status.UP:
            try:
                response = self.session.get(self.url, timeout=self.timeout)
                if expected_string and expected_string in response.text:
                    health_data['content_healthy'] = True
                elif expected_string:
                    health_data['content_healthy'] = False
                    health_data['status'] = Status.DEGRADED.value
            except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
                health_data['error'] = str(e)
                health_data['status'] = Status.DOWN.value
        
        return health_data

    def generate_report(self, health_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
        """
        Generate a comprehensive JSON report of the health check.
        """
        return json.dumps(health_data, indent=2)

# Example usage for advanced monitoring
if __name__ == "__main__":
    advanced_monitor = AdvancedWebsiteMonitor("https://eth-gas.org")
    
    # Check for specific content that should be present
    health_data = advanced_monitor.check_content(expected_string="gas prices")
    
    report = advanced_monitor.generate_report(health_data)
    print(report)
    
    # Log the comprehensive report
    logger.info(f"Advanced health check report: {report}")
```

```python
# Integration with popular monitoring services
import os
from slack_sdk import WebClient
from slack_sdk.errors import SlackApiError

class MonitoringServiceIntegration:
    def __init__(self):
        self.slack_client = WebClient(token=os.environ.get('SLACK_BOT_TOKEN')) if os.environ.get('SLACK_BOT_TOKEN') else None

    def send_slack_alert(self, message: str, channel: str = "#website-monitoring"):
        """
        Send alert to Slack channel.
        """
        if not self.slack_client:
            logger.warning("Slack bot token not configured. Skipping Slack alert.")
            return
        
        try:
            response = self.slack_client.chat_postMessage(
                channel=channel,
                text=message
            )
            logger.info(f"Slack alert sent: {response['ts']}")
        except SlackApiError as e:
            logger.error(f"Error sending Slack alert: {e.response['error']}")

    def send_alert_if_down(self, monitor: WebsiteMonitor, channel: str = "#website-monitoring"):
        """
        Check website and send alert if it's down.
        """
        result = monitor.check_availability()
        
        if result.status != Status.UP:
            message = f"🚨 Website Alert: {monitor.url} is {result.status.value}\n"
            message += f"Response Time: {result.response_time:.2f}s\n"
            if result.error:
                message += f"Error: {result.error}"
            self.send_slack_alert(message, channel)

# Example integration usage
if __name__ == "__main__":
    monitor = WebsiteMonitor("https://eth-gas.org")
    service_integration = MonitoringServiceIntegration()
    
    # Check and alert if down
    service_integration.send_alert_if_down(monitor)
```

# Configuration and multiple website monitoring
import yaml
from typing import List

class MultiWebsiteMonitor:
    def __init__(self, config_path: str = "monitoring_config.yaml"):
        self.config = self.load_config(config_path)
        self.monitors = []
        self.setup_monitors()

    def load_config(self, config_path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Load monitoring configuration from YAML file.
        """
        try:
            with open(config_path, 'r') as file:
                return yaml.safe_load(file)
        except FileNotFoundError:
            logger.error(f"Configuration file {config_path} not found.")
            return {'websites': []}
        except yaml.YAMLError as e:
            logger.error(f"Error parsing YAML configuration: {e}")
            return {'websites': []}

    def setup_monitors(self):
        """
        Setup monitors for all configured websites.
        """
        for site_config in self.config.get('websites', []):
            monitor = WebsiteMonitor(
                url=site_config['url'],
                timeout=site_config.get('timeout', 10)
            )
            self.monitors.append({
                'monitor': monitor,
                'name': site_config.get('name', site_config['url']),
                'expected_content': site_config.get('expected_content')
            })

                'url': monitor.url,
                'status': result.status.value,
                'response_time': result.response_time,
                'status_code': result.status_code,
                'error': result.error
            }
            
            results.append(result_data)
            
            # Log individual result
            if result.status != Status.UP:
                logger.warning(f"Website {monitor_info['name']} ({monitor.url}) is {result.status.value}")
        
        return results

```python
The API endpoint used is: https://pythclaims.web.app/api/collections

Note: The API might have rate limits or require authentication. This script includes
basic error handling for common issues like network errors or unexpected responses.

Dependencies:
    requests: To make HTTP requests. Install via `pip install requests`

Usage:
    Run the script and it will print the data for the specified collection.
"""

import requests
import json

# Configuration
API_URL = "https://pythclaims.web.app/api/collections"
COLLECTION_NAME = "Degenerate Ape Academy"  # Replace with desired collection name

def fetch_nft_collection_data(api_url, collection_name):
    """
    Fetches data for a specific NFT collection from the PythClaims API.

    Args:
        api_url (str): The URL of the API endpoint.
        collection_name (str): The name of the NFT collection to fetch.

    Returns:
        dict: The JSON response as a dictionary if successful, None otherwise.

    Raises:
        requests.exceptions.RequestException: For network-related errors.
    """
    try:
        response = requests.get(api_url)
        response.raise_for_status()  # Raises an HTTPError for bad responses (4xx or 5xx)

        # Parse the JSON response
        data = response.json()

        # Check if the response is a list of collections
        if isinstance(data, list):
            # Find the collection by name
            for collection in data:
                if collection.get('name') == collection_name:
                    return collection
            print(f"Collection '{collection_name}' not found.")
            return None
        else:
            print("Unexpected API response format.")
            return None

    except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
        print(f"Network error occurred: {e}")
        return None
    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
        print(f"Error decoding JSON response: {e}")
        return None

if __name__ == "__main__":

    if collection_data:

        print(json.dumps(collection_data, indent=2))
    else:
        print("Failed to fetch collection data.")
```

```python
import requests
import json

def fetch_floor_prices():
    """
    Fetches the current floor prices for Mad Lads and Transdimensional Fox Federation NFTs
    from the temdaocom.web.app API.
    
    Returns:
        dict: A dictionary containing the floor prices for both collections.
    """
    url = "https://temdaocom.web.app/api/collections"
    headers = {
        "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36"
    }
    
    try:
        response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=10)
        response.raise_for_status()
        
        data = response.json()
        
        # Extract floor prices for the desired collections
        collections = data.get("collections", [])
        mad_lads_floor = None
        fox_federation_floor = None
        
        for collection in collections:
            name = collection.get("name", "").lower()
            floor_price = collection.get("floorPrice")
            
            if "mad lads" in name:
                mad_lads_floor = floor_price
            elif "transdimensional fox federation" in name:
                fox_federation_floor = floor_price
        
        return {
            "Mad Lads": mad_lads_floor,
            "Transdimensional Fox Federation": fox_federation_floor
        }
        
    except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
        print(f"Error fetching data from API: {e}")
        return None
    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
        print(f"Error parsing JSON response: {e}")
        return None

def display_floor_prices(prices):
    """
    Displays the floor prices in a formatted manner.
    
    Args:
        prices (dict): Dictionary containing floor prices for the collections.
    """
    if not prices:
        print("Failed to retrieve floor prices.")
        return
        
    print("Current Floor Prices:")
    print("---------------------")
    for collection, price in prices.items():
        if price is not None:
            print(f"{collection}: {price} SOL")
        else:
            print(f"{collection}: Price not available")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    floor_prices = fetch_floor_prices()
    display_floor_prices(floor_prices)
```

```python
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import smtplib
from email.mime.text import MIMEText
from email.mime.multipart import MIMEMultipart
import logging
import time
from typing import Optional, Dict, Any

# Configure logging
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format='%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s')
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

class ZKOutdoorProductChecker:
    def __init__(self, product_url: str, recipient_email: str, sender_email: str, sender_password: str):
        """
        Initialize the product checker with product URL and email details.
        
        Args:
            product_url (str): The URL of the product to check.
            recipient_email (str): Email address to send notifications to.
            sender_email (str): Email address to send from.
            sender_password (str): Password for the sender email.
        """
        self.product_url = product_url
        self.recipient_email = recipient_email
        self.sender_email = sender_email
        self.sender_password = sender_password
        self.headers = {
            'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/58.0.3029.110 Safari/537.3'
        }

    def fetch_product_page(self) -> Optional[str]:
        """
        Fetch the product page HTML content.
        
        Returns:
            Optional[str]: HTML content as string if successful, None otherwise.
        """
        try:
            response = requests.get(self.product_url, headers=self.headers, timeout=10)
            response.raise_for_status()
            return response.text
        except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f"Error fetching product page: {e}")
            return None

    def parse_product_details(self, html: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Parse the product details from HTML.
        
        Args:
            html (str): HTML content of the product page.
            
        Returns:
            Optional[Dict[str, Any]]: Dictionary containing product details (availability, shipping, etc.) if successful, None otherwise.
        """
        try:
            soup = BeautifulSoup(html, 'html.parser')
            
            # Extract product title
            title_tag = soup.find('h1', class_='product_title')
            title = title_tag.get_text(strip=True) if title_tag else "Unknown Product"
            
            # Extract availability - this selector might need adjustment based on actual site structure
            availability_tag = soup.find('p', class_='stock in-stock')
            if not availability_tag:
                availability_tag = soup.find('p', class_='stock out-of-stock')
            availability = availability_tag.get_text(strip=True) if availability_tag else "Availability not found"
            
            # Extract price - this selector might need adjustment
            price_tag = soup.find('p', class_='price')
            price = price_tag.get_text(strip=True) if price_tag else "Price not found"
            
            # Extract shipping info - this might be in a different section; adjust as needed
            shipping_info = "Shipping information not found"
            # Example: look for a shipping tab or section
            shipping_tab = soup.find('div', class_='woocommerce-tabs wc-tabs-wrapper')
            if shipping_tab:
                shipping_items = shipping_tab.find_all('li')
                for item in shipping_items:
                    if 'shipping' in item.get_text().lower():
                        shipping_info = item.get_text(strip=True)
                        break
            
            return {
                'title': title,
                'availability': availability,
                'price': price,
                'shipping_info': shipping_info,
                'url': self.product_url
            }
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Error parsing product details: {e}")
            return None

    def send_email_notification(self, product_details: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
        """
        Send an email notification with product details.
        
        Args:
            product_details (Dict[str, Any]): Dictionary containing product details.
            
        Returns:
            bool: True if email was sent successfully, False otherwise.
        """
        try:
            msg = MIMEMultipart()
            msg['From'] = self.sender_email
            msg['To'] = self.recipient_email
            msg['Subject'] = f"Product Inquiry: {product_details['title']}"
            
            body = f"""
            Product: {product_details['title']}
            Availability: {product_details['availability']}
            Price: {product_details['price']}
            Shipping Info: {product_details['shipping_info']}
            URL: {product_details['url']}
            """
            msg.attach(MIMEText(body, 'plain'))
            
            server = smtplib.SMTP('smtp.gmail.com', 587)  # Adjust SMTP server as needed
            server.starttls()
            server.login(self.sender_email, self.sender_password)
            text = msg.as_string()
            server.sendmail(self.sender_email, self.recipient_email, text)
            server.quit()
            
            logger.info("Email notification sent successfully.")
            return True
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Error sending email: {e}")
            return False

    def check_product(self) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Main method to check product availability and shipping.
        
        Returns:
            Optional[Dict[str, Any]]: Product details if successful, None otherwise.
        """
        html = self.fetch_product_page()
        if html is None:
            return None
        
        product_details = self.parse_product_details(html)
        if product_details is None:
            return None
        
        return product_details

def main():
    # Configuration - replace with actual values
    PRODUCT_URL = "https://zkoutdoor.pk/product/divider-product-example/"  # Example URL
    RECIPIENT_EMAIL = "customer_service@example.com"  # Customer service email
    SENDER_EMAIL = "your_email@gmail.com"  # Your email
    SENDER_PASSWORD = "your_password"  # Your email password or app-specific password
    
    # Initialize product checker
    checker = ZKOutdoorProductChecker(PRODUCT_URL, RECIPIENT_EMAIL, SENDER_EMAIL, SENDER_PASSWORD)
    
    # Check product
    product_details = checker.check_product()
    if product_details:
        logger.info(f"Product details: {product_details}")
        # Send email notification
        checker.send_email_notification(product_details)
    else:
        logger.error("Failed to retrieve product details.")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Note: This script is designed to scrape product information from a WooCommerce-based site (like zkoutdoor.pk) and send an email notification. However, the actual HTML structure may vary, so the selectors in `parse_product_details` might need adjustment. Also, for sending emails, it uses Gmail's SMTP server; if using another provider, change the SMTP settings accordingly. Ensure that the sender email has allowed less secure apps or uses an app-specific password if required.

prompts.prompt: Write a function in R to scrape travel-related data from websites similar to https://bienbonvoyage.com for market research on luxury adventures.
prompts.generated_code:
```r
library(rvest)
library(httr)
library(xml2)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)

scrape_luxury_adventures <- function(urls, timeout = 10, max_retries = 3) {
  # Function to scrape luxury adventure travel data from given URLs
  #
  # Args:
  #   urls: Character vector of URLs to scrape
  #   timeout: Timeout for HTTP requests in seconds
  #   max_retries: Maximum number of retries for failed requests
  #
  # Returns:
  #   A data frame containing scraped travel data
  
  # Validate input
  if (!is.character(urls) || length(urls) == 0) {
    stop("URLs must be a non-empty character vector")
  }
  
  # User agent to mimic browser request
  user_agent <- "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/91.0.4472.124 Safari/537.36"
  
  # Function to safely read HTML with retries and error handling
  safe_read_html <- function(url, retries = max_retries) {
    for (i in 1:retries) {
      tryCatch({
        response <- GET(url, user_agent(user_agent), timeout(timeout))
        
        # Check if request was successful
        if (status_code(response) == 200) {
          content <- read_html(response)
          return(content)
        } else {
          warning(paste("HTTP error", status_code(response), "for URL:", url))
        }
      }, error = function(e) {
        warning(paste("Attempt", i, "failed for URL:", url, "- Error:", e$message))
        if (i == retries) {
          return(NULL)
        }
        Sys.sleep(2) # Wait before retrying
      })
    }
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Function to extract data from a single page
  extract_page_data <- function(html) {
    if (is.null(html)) return(NULL)
    
    tryCatch({
      # Extract title
      title <- html %>%
        html_node("h1.entry-title, h1.post-title, h1.title, .title h1, h1") %>%
        html_text(trim = TRUE)
      
      if (length(title) == 0) title <- NA
      
      # Extract description
      description <- html %>%
        html_node("meta[name='description'], meta[property='og:description']") %>%
        html_attr("content")
      
      if (length(description) == 0) {
        description <- html %>%
          html_node("div.entry-content, div.post-content, article p, .content p") %>%
          html_text(trim = TRUE) %>%
          str_replace_all("\\s+", " ") %>%
          substr(1, 300)
      }
      
      # Extract price information
      price <- html %>%
        html_nodes("span.price, .price, .amount, .cost, .price-tag") %>%
        html_text(trim = TRUE) %>%
        paste(collapse = " | ")
      
      if (price == "") price <- NA
      
      # Extract location/destination
      location <- html %>%
        html_nodes(".location, .destination, .place, .country, .region") %>%
        html_text(trim = TRUE) %>%
        paste(collapse = " | ")
      
      if (location == "") location <- NA
      
      # Extract duration
      duration <- html %>%
        html_nodes(".duration, .length, .days, .nights") %>%
        html_text(trim = TRUE) %>%
        paste(collapse = " | ")
      
      if (duration == "") duration <- NA
      
      # Extract activities
      activities <- html %>%
        html_nodes(".activities, .activity, .tags, .keywords") %>%
        html_text(trim = TRUE) %>%
        paste(collapse = " | ")
      
      if (activities == "") activities <- NA
      
      # Extract rating if available
      rating <- html %>%
        html_nodes(".rating, .review-score, .stars, .score") %>%
        html_text(trim = TRUE) %>%
        str_extract("[0-9.]+") %>%
        as.numeric()
      
      if (length(rating) == 0) rating <- NA
      
      # Create data frame with extracted data
      data.frame(
        Title = title,
        Description = description,
        Price = price,
        Location = location,
        Duration = duration,
        Activities = activities,
        Rating = rating,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }, error = function(e) {
      warning(paste("Error extracting data from page:", e$message))
      return(NULL)
    })
  }
  
  # Scrape all URLs
  results <- list()
  
  for (i in seq_along(urls)) {
    url <- urls[i]
    cat("Scraping URL", i, "of", length(urls), ":", url, "\n")
    
    html_content <- safe_read_html(url)
    page_data <- extract_page_data(html_content)
    
    if (!is.null(page_data)) {
      page_data$URL <- url
      results[[i]] <- page_data
    } else {
      warning(paste("Failed to scrape URL:", url))
      # Add empty row with URL for failed attempts
      results[[i]] <- data.frame(
        Title = NA,
        Description = NA,
        Price = NA,
        Location = NA,
        Duration = NA,
        Activities = NA,
        Rating = NA,
        URL = url,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
    
    # Be polite - delay between requests
    Sys.sleep(1)
  }
  
  # Combine all results into a single data frame
  final_df <- bind_rows(results)
  
  return(final_df)
}

# Example usage:
# urls <- c("https://bienbonvoyage.com/adventure1", "https://bienbonvoyage.com/adventure2")
# travel_data <- scrape_luxury_adventures(urls)
# write.csv(travel_data, "luxury_adventures_data.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
